seoTitle: McIntyre vs McKenzie Hill — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between McIntyre and McKenzie Hill. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: McIntyre vs McKenzie Hill: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in McIntyre and McKenzie Hill.
heading: McIntyre vs McKenzie Hill — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between McIntyre, Victoria and McKenzie Hill, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: McIntyre has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to McKenzie Hill (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is McIntyre or McKenzie Hill safer from flooding?
faq.answer: McIntyre has a flood risk score of 22/100, while McKenzie Hill scores 25/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: McIntyre scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. McKenzie Hill scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in McIntyre or McKenzie Hill?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. McIntyre has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.45% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
